text:
COLON, SIGMOID, BIOPSY

Clinical History: Patient is a 58-year-old with rectal bleeding.

Final Diagnosis: Colonic mucosa with mild chronic inflammation. No dysplasia or malignancy.

BREAST, LEFT, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

Clinical History: Jane Smith, DOB 03/14/1971, MRN 8473920, presents with a 1.5 cm palpable mass. Referred by Dr. Robert Chen, MGH Breast Surgery, 617-555-0182.

Final Diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 2. ER positive, PR positive, HER2 negative.

LUNG, RIGHT UPPER LOBE, WEDGE RESECTION

Clinical History: Patient is a 67-year-old with a history of smoking and a 2.1 cm right upper lobe mass on CT.

Final Diagnosis: Invasive adenocarcinoma, acinar predominant, grade 2. Margins negative. pT1c Nx Mx.

PROSTATE, NEEDLE BIOPSY

Clinical History: 71-year-old male with rising PSA (8.2 ng/mL). Previous biopsy negative.

Final Diagnosis:
- Cores 1–3 (right base): Benign prostatic tissue.
- Cores 4–6 (right mid): Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 3+4=7, involving 2 of 3 cores, 20% involvement.
- Cores 7–12 (left side): Benign prostatic tissue and benign prostatic hyperplasia.

SKIN, BACK, SHAVE BIOPSY

Patient: [PATIENT NAME], seen at Memorial Hospital on 2019-04-12. Medical record: 00294710.

Final Diagnosis: Superficial basal cell carcinoma. Margins positive at base. Re-excision recommended.

GASTRIC BIOPSY — ANTRUM

Clinical History: 49-year-old with dyspepsia and positive urea breath test.

Final Diagnosis: Chronic active gastritis with Helicobacter pylori. No intestinal metaplasia. No dysplasia.

THYROID, FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION, RIGHT LOBE NODULE

Submitting physician: Dr. Amanda Torres, Endocrinology, [HOSPITAL NAME], email: atorres@hospital.org
Patient contact: 555-204-9917

Diagnosis: Bethesda category V — Suspicious for papillary thyroid carcinoma. Recommend surgical excision.

LIVER, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

Clinical History: 55-year-old with known hepatitis C cirrhosis and a 2.8 cm arterially enhancing lesion on MRI.

Final Diagnosis: Hepatocellular carcinoma, moderately differentiated. Background liver shows established cirrhosis (Metavir F4).

KIDNEY, RIGHT, PARTIAL NEPHRECTOMY

Clinical History: Incidental 3.2 cm right renal mass in a 62-year-old.

Final Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, ISUP grade 2. Tumor confined to kidney (pT1b). Margins negative. No sarcomatoid differentiation. No necrosis.

CERVICAL BIOPSY AND ENDOCERVICAL CURETTAGE

Patient ID: 19-C-04421. Specimen collected: January 8, 2019. Patient date of birth: July 22, 1989. Ordering provider: Dr. Lisa Park, 404-555-0093.

Final Diagnosis:
- Cervical biopsy: High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL/CIN3).
- Endocervical curettage: Fragments of high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion.
